turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#0b2340" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#071427" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="cloudGrad" x1="0" x2="1" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#6b6b6b" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#3c3c3c" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="boltGrad" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff06a" />
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffd24a" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff9f1c" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="glow" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="8" result="blur" />
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="blur" />
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic" />
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#bgGrad)" />

  <!-- distant subtle cloud shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="300" rx="140" ry="40" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.12)" />

  <!-- main cloud -->
  <g transform="translate(0, -20)">
    <path d="M120 300
             c-30 0 -50 -28 -40 -58
             c10 -34 46 -48 80 -36
             c6 -40 48 -68 92 -56
             c10 -30 56 -46 92 -18
             c34 -8 80 10 92 46
             c18 44 -6 86 -52 92
             H120z"
          fill="url(#cloudGrad)" />
    <!-- soft top bumps to make cloud rounder -->
    <circle cx="160" cy="230" r="28" fill="url(#cloudGrad)" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="200" r="36" fill="url(#cloudGrad)" />
    <circle cx="310" cy="190" r="48" fill="url(#cloudGrad)" />
    <circle cx="380" cy="220" r="34" fill="url(#cloudGrad)" />
  </g>

  <!-- lightning bolt with glow -->
  <g filter="url(#glow)">
    <path d="M320 270
             L260 360
             L300 360
             L240 460
             L360 340
             L320 340
             L360 270
             Z"
          fill="url(#boltGrad)" stroke="#d37a00" stroke-width="3" stroke-linejoin="round" />
  </g>

  <!-- small spark lines near bolt tip -->
  <g opacity="0.85" stroke="#ffd96b" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round">
    <path d="M352 264 L372 248" />
    <path d="M344 280 L368 292" />
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for thunder**

I need to create JSON with an SVG string representing thunder. The design should include a dark gray cloud, zigzag yellow lightning bolts, and maybe raindrops, all without text or numbers. I have to ensure the SVG fits a 512x512 canvas and uses a dark blue gradient for the background. I'll also need to escape quotes in the JSON and consider adding a glow effect for the lightning using filters. Keeping it simple and valid is key. Let's get started on crafting that SVG!**Designing SVG Elements**

I'm working on creating an SVG for thunder using specific elements. First, I need to structure it with the right dimensions and a background gradient. messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
